subject: Add 'PolicySession.insertDrools' method
commit_message:
Add 'PolicySession.insertDrools' method

This change includes feature hooks, so a "smart insert" could forward
the object to a remote host, and do the insert there. The methods 'insert'
and 'insertAll' in 'PolicyContainer' now make use of this smart insert.

Change-Id: I69f0e874b6fda09d1f457e4353e4b30d63696210
Issue-ID: POLICY-2160
Signed-off-by: Straubs, Ralph (rs8887) <rs8887@att.com>

Patch Set 1:

Can you mark the JIRA in progress - as well as ensure it really should be standalone vs a sub-task within a user-story and/or epic. Thanks.

Patch Set 1:

I think PolicyContainer.insert(..) and PolicyContainer.insertAll(..) should be updated to call PolicySession.insertDrools(..) to make sure the interception mechanism happens for all insertion paths.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Can you mark the JIRA in progress - as well as ensure it really should be standalone vs a sub-task within a user-story and/or epic. Thanks.

I have marked it "in-progress".

This change was actually prompted by a bug in an ECOMP control loop -- this capability is needed to fix the problem. The code change here makes it a generic operation, so it isn't just something in server pool. It is similar to the new lock API in that respect.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I think PolicyContainer.insert(..) and PolicyContainer.insertAll(..) should be updated to call PolicySession.insertDrools(..) to make sure the interception mechanism happens for all insertion paths.

Do we want to support both direct and smart inserts, or should only the smart insert be provided. Would this affect incoming UEB/DMAAP inserts as well? In server pool, I am currently intercepting 'PolicyControllerFeatureApi.beforeOffer' in order to forward messages -- if incoming UEB/DMAAP messages did smart inserts, things would still work without the 'beforeOffer' intercept.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1:
>

> > Can you mark the JIRA in progress - as well as ensure it really should be standalone vs a sub-task within a user-story and/or epic. Thanks.

> I have marked it "in-progress".
> 
> This change was actually prompted by a bug in an ECOMP control loop -- this capability is needed to fix the problem. The code change here makes it a generic operation, so it isn't just something in server pool. It is similar to the new lock API in that respect.

Got it thanks!
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1:
 > >
 > > I think PolicyContainer.insert(..) and PolicyContainer.insertAll(..)
 > should be updated to call PolicySession.insertDrools(..) to make
 > sure the interception mechanism happens for all insertion paths.
 > 
 > Do we want to support both direct and smart inserts, or should only
 > the smart insert be provided. Would this affect incoming UEB/DMAAP
 > inserts as well? In server pool, I am currently intercepting
 > 'PolicyControllerFeatureApi.beforeOffer' in order to forward
 > messages -- if incoming UEB/DMAAP messages did smart inserts,
 > things would still work without the 'beforeOffer' intercept.

The changes I am suggesting will provide hooks to all inserts, so both direct and smart, so all invokations to kiesession.insert(..) will have the possibility to be intercepted regardless.   The "smart" vs. "non-smart" insert decision will be up to the underlying feature to make that distinction.    So, this will affect all paths including the UEB/DMaaP and inserts through telemetry APIs.   It seems that the beforeOffer won't be necessary in server pool may be redundant then, unless you want to do something special directly withthe PolicyController object that is passed in (although could be inferred too in a slightly more complex way from PolicySession).    I think the main benefit for the change would be consistency, and maintenance, as having all kie inserts in the same place in those 2 methods in the PolicySession, instead of in several places, ie. PolicyContainer and PolicySession.
Uploaded patch set 2.
